description: §23.5.5 — the presence-nudge lint fires for a `_{}` INLINE foreign-code block (§23.2), not just the `use foreign:` sidecar the existing cases exercise. An inline value-returning `_={ … }=` block sits inside a `<program lang="ts">` with NO `capabilities=` on any ancestor → governed by the empty set → W-FOREIGN-UNDECLARED-CAPABILITY (Info). Unlike the sidecar cases, the inline `_{}` form does NOT co-fire E-FOREIGN-SIDECAR-NOMINAL (that Nominal error is sidecar-runtime-specific), so this is the cleanest single-construct proof of the lint. Twin of `inline-block-covered`.
rationale: §23.5.5: the compiler SHALL emit W-FOREIGN-UNDECLARED-CAPABILITY (Info) when a foreign-code construct — a `_{}` block (§23.2), WASM sigil (§23.3), or `use foreign:` sidecar (§23.4) — is governed by an empty capability set. Here the `_={ … }=` block has no ancestor `capabilities=`, so the empty-set nudge fires. §23.5.5 opacity bound: it checks declaration-PRESENCE only. No E-SCOPE-001, no E-FOREIGN-CAPABILITY-UNKNOWN.
